column3: English
column4: Breakdown
column5: Examples
column6: Note

column3: red-faced / flushed
column4: 面 (face) 红 (red) 耳 (ear) 赤 (flushed)
column5:
他们俩为一点小事吵得面红耳赤。 / The two of them argued over a trivial matter until they were red in the face.
被老师点名回答时，她紧张得面红耳赤。 / When called on by the teacher, she got flushed with nervousness.
听到那句玩笑话，他面红耳赤地低下了头。 / Hearing that joke, he lowered his head, blushing.
column6: Often from embarrassment, anger, or argument.

column3: wrapped in white / covered in snow
column4: 银 (silver) 装 (dress) 素 (white) 裹 (wrap)
column5:
一夜大雪过后，整座城市银装素裹。 / After a night of heavy snow, the whole city was wrapped in white.
山顶银装素裹，美得像一幅画。 / The mountaintop was covered in snow, beautiful as a painting.
清晨推开窗，外面已是银装素裹。 / Opening the window at dawn, outside was already blanketed in snow.
column6: Poetic description of a snowy white landscape.

column3: after bitterness comes sweetness
column4: 苦 (bitter) 尽 (end) 甘 (sweet) 来 (come)
column5:
奋斗了这么多年，终于苦尽甘来了。 / After struggling for so many years, the good times have finally come.
她相信只要坚持下去，总会苦尽甘来。 / She believes that if she keeps going, sweetness will follow the bitterness.
创业头几年很辛苦，现在算是苦尽甘来。 / The first years of the startup were rough; now we're finally reaping the rewards.
column6: Hardship eventually leads to good results.

column3: have you been well since we last met?
column4: 别 (part) 来 (since) 无 (no) 恙 (illness)
column5:
好久不见，别来无恙吧？ / Long time no see — have you been well?
老同学，别来无恙，最近怎么样？ / Old classmate, hope you've been well — how have things been?
他发来一条短信：别来无恙。 / He sent a message: hope you've been doing okay.
column6: Old-fashioned/literary greeting. Good for classical flavor, not normal daily greeting.

column3: through and through / complete
column4: 彻 (through) 头 (head) 尾 (tail)
column5:
这是一个彻头彻尾的谎言。 / This is an out-and-out lie.
他是个彻头彻尾的乐观主义者。 / He's an optimist through and through.
这种做法彻头彻尾地违反了规定。 / This practice completely violates the rules.
column6: Means completely or downright, often before a negative noun/adjective.

column3: brought to perfection
column4: 炉 (furnace) 火 (fire) 纯 (pure) 青 (blue-green)
column5:
他的厨艺已经到了炉火纯青的地步。 / His cooking skills have reached the level of perfection.
经过多年练习，她的钢琴技艺炉火纯青。 / After years of practice, her piano technique is masterful.
这位老师傅的手艺真是炉火纯青。 / This old craftsman's skill is truly perfected.
column6: Describes a skill that has reached a very high level.

column3: courtesy should be reciprocated
column4: 礼 (courtesy) 尚 (esteem) 往 (go) 来 (come)
column5:
中国人讲究礼尚往来，他送了礼，我也得回。 / Chinese culture values reciprocity — he gave a gift, so I have to return one.
礼尚往来嘛，下次该我请客了。 / Courtesy goes both ways — next time it's my treat.
朋友之间礼尚往来很正常。 / Reciprocating between friends is perfectly normal.
column6: Used for social reciprocity: favors, gifts, respect, politeness.

column3: family happiness
column4: 天 (heaven) 伦 (relations) 之 (of) 乐 (joy)
column5:
退休后，他在家享受天伦之乐。 / After retiring, he enjoys family life at home.
周末一家人聚在一起，尽享天伦之乐。 / On weekends the family gathers and enjoys time together.
工作再忙，也别忘了天伦之乐。 / No matter how busy work gets, don't forget family happiness.
column6: Literary phrase for joy from family relationships.

column3: to rack one’s brains
column4: 绞 (wring) 尽 (exhaust) 脑 (brain) 汁 (juice)
column5:
我绞尽脑汁也想不出答案。 / I racked my brain but still couldn't think of the answer.
为了这个项目，他绞尽脑汁。 / He's been racking his brains over this project.
她绞尽脑汁想给孩子起个好名字。 / She racked her brain trying to find a good name for her child.
column6: Literally to squeeze out all the brain juice; used when thinking very hard.

column3: completely different
column4: 截 (cut) 然 (so) 不 (not) 同 (same)
column5:
南方和北方的饮食习惯截然不同。 / Eating habits in the south and north are completely different.
他们俩的性格截然不同，却是好朋友。 / Their personalities are totally different, yet they're close friends.
这次的结果和上次截然不同。 / This result is completely different from last time.
column6: Useful idiom-like phrase; fairly practical.

column3: like-minded
column4: 志 (aspiration) 同 (same) 道 (path) 合 (match)
column5:
我们是志同道合的朋友，所以合作很顺利。 / We're like-minded friends, so working together goes smoothly.
在这里我遇到了不少志同道合的人。 / Here I've met many people who share my goals.
他在找志同道合的合伙人一起创业。 / He's looking for a like-minded partner to start a business with.
column6: Used for people who share goals, ideals, or values.

column3: deep affection and friendship
column4: 深 (deep) 情 (feeling) 厚 (thick) 谊 (friendship)
column5:
感谢大家多年来的深情厚谊。 / Thank you all for your deep friendship over the years.
这份深情厚谊我一辈子都记得。 / I'll remember this deep affection my whole life.
两国人民有着深情厚谊。 / The two peoples share deep friendship.
column6: Formal/literary expression for a strong bond.

column3: a true soulmate is hard to find
column4: 知 (know) 音 (tone) 难 (hard) 觅 (seek)
column5:
人到中年，越发觉得知音难觅。 / In middle age, you feel even more that true friends are hard to find.
他感慨道：知音难觅啊。 / He sighed: a real soulmate is hard to come by.
知音难觅，遇到一个要好好珍惜。 / Soulmates are rare — cherish the one you find.
column6: Literary phrase about finding someone who truly understands you.

column3: to add fuel to the fire
column4: 火 (fire) 上 (on) 浇 (pour) 油 (oil)
column5:
他正在气头上，你这么说简直是火上浇油。 / He's furious right now — saying that is just adding fuel to the fire.
媒体的报道无疑是火上浇油。 / The media coverage was undoubtedly throwing gasoline on the fire.
别在他们吵架的时候火上浇油了。 / Don't make things worse while they're arguing.
column6: Used when making an already bad situation worse.

column3: burning with anger / seething
column4: 怒 (anger) 火 (fire) 中 (within) 烧 (burn)
column5:
听到这话，他顿时怒火中烧。 / Hearing this, he was instantly seething with rage.
看到孩子被欺负，她怒火中烧。 / Seeing her child bullied, she was burning with anger.
他强压着怒火中烧的感觉，没有发作。 / He suppressed his burning anger and didn't lash out.
column6: Literary-sounding expression for intense anger.

column2: chī yí qiàn zhǎng yí zhì
column3: learn from a setback
column4: 吃 (eat) 一 (one) 堑 (pit) 长 (gain) 智 (wisdom)
column5:
这次失败了没关系，吃一堑长一智嘛。 / It's okay to fail this time — every setback teaches you something.
他吃一堑长一智，再也不轻易相信陌生人了。 / He learned his lesson and won't easily trust strangers anymore.
吃一堑，长一智，下次小心点就好。 / You live and learn — just be careful next time.
column6: Proverb: one fall into a pit, one gain in wisdom.

column3: practice makes perfect
column4: 熟 (familiar) 能 (can) 生 (produce) 巧 (skill)
column5:
别担心，熟能生巧，多练几次就好了。 / Don't worry — practice makes perfect, just keep practicing.
做菜也是熟能生巧的事。 / Cooking is also something that improves with practice.
他打字快是因为熟能生巧。 / He types fast because of constant practice.
column6: Very useful proverb/idiom.

column3: great talent matures late
column4: 大 (great) 器 (vessel) 晚 (late) 成 (achieve)
column5:
别着急，有些人就是大器晚成。 / Don't rush — some people simply bloom late.
他四十岁才出名，真是大器晚成。 / He became famous at forty — a true late bloomer.
妈妈安慰我说，我是大器晚成型的。 / Mom comforted me, saying I'm the late-blooming type.
column6: Used for people who succeed later after long development.

column3: hardship produces talent
column4: 逆 (adverse) 境 (situation) 出 (produce) 人 (person) 才 (talent)
column5:
古人说逆境出人才，吃苦不是坏事。 / The ancients said hardship produces talent — suffering isn't a bad thing.
他相信逆境出人才，所以从不抱怨。 / He believes adversity breeds talent, so he never complains.
逆境出人才，许多名人都经历过艰难岁月。 / Hardship produces talent — many famous people have been through tough times.
column6: Proverb-like motivational saying.

column3: constant effort brings sure success
column4: 功 (effort) 到 (arrive) 自 (self) 然 (so) 成 (succeed)
column5:
别急着看结果，功到自然成。 / Don't rush the results — success comes with sustained effort.
她相信功到自然成，每天坚持练习。 / She believes effort pays off and practices every day.
学语言急不来，功到自然成。 / You can't rush language learning — effort eventually pays off.
column6: Proverb: when the work is done, success follows naturally.

column2: bú pà màn jiù pà zhàn
column3: better slow progress than standing still
column4: 不 (not) 怕 (fear) 慢 (slow) 就 (only) 站 (stand still)
column5:
学习就是不怕慢，就怕站，每天进步一点。 / In learning, slow is fine — stopping isn't; just improve daily.
减肥不怕慢，就怕站，关键是坚持。 / Slow weight loss is okay — the key is not stopping.
他常说不怕慢就怕站，所以每天都跑一点。 / He often says slow is fine but stopping isn't, so he runs a little every day.
column6: Proverb encouraging steady progress.

column3: skilled in both pen and sword
column4: 文 (literary) 武 (martial) 双 (both) 全 (complete)
column5:
他不但会写文章，还会打篮球，真是文武双全。 / He writes well and plays basketball — truly well-rounded.
古代的将军很多都是文武双全。 / Many ancient generals were skilled in both literature and combat.
她在公司里能写能干，文武双全。 / At the company she can both write and execute — all-around capable.
column6: Classical/literary praise for someone capable in both intellectual and martial areas.

column1: 谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后
column2: qiān xū shǐ rén jìn bù jiāo ào shǐ rén luò hòu
column3: modesty helps one progress; pride makes one fall behind
column4: 谦 (modest) 虚 (humble) 使 (cause) 人 (person) 进 (advance) 步 (step) 骄 (proud) 傲 (arrogant) 落 (fall) 后 (behind)
column5:
老师常说：谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。 / The teacher often says: modesty leads to progress, pride leads to falling behind.
成绩好也别骄傲，谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。 / Good grades aren't a reason to be proud — humility brings progress.
他时刻提醒自己谦虚使人进步，骄傲使人落后。 / He constantly reminds himself that pride leads to backsliding.
column6: Well-known saying associated with self-improvement and humility.

column2: jūn jūn chén chén fù fù zǐ zi
column3: rulers should act as rulers, ministers as ministers, fathers as fathers, sons as sons
column4: 君 (ruler) 臣 (minister) 父 (father) 子 (son)
column5:
孔子讲究君君臣臣父父子子，强调各守本分。 / Confucius emphasized everyone fulfilling their proper role.
古代社会非常重视君君臣臣父父子子的伦理。 / Ancient society highly valued the ethics of everyone in their proper role.
这本书讨论了君君臣臣父父子子的现代意义。 / This book discusses the modern meaning of Confucian role ethics.
column6: Classical Confucian phrase about proper social roles.

column2: yǒu zhì zhě shì jìng chéng
column3: where there is a will, there is a way
column4: 有 (have) 志 (will) 者 (one who) 事 (matter) 竟 (ultimately) 成 (succeed)
column5:
别放弃，有志者事竟成。 / Don't give up — where there's a will, there's a way.
他从小就相信有志者事竟成。 / Since childhood he's believed that determination leads to success.
有志者事竟成，只要努力就有希望。 / If you have the will, you'll succeed — there's hope as long as you try.
column6: Common motivational proverb.

column1: 舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马
column2: shě de yì shēn guǎ gǎn bǎ huáng dì lā xià mǎ
column3: one who fears not being cut to pieces dares to unhorse the emperor
column4: 舍 (give up) 得 (willing) 一 (one) 身 (body) 剐 (cut to pieces) 敢 (dare) 把 (take) 皇 (imperial) 帝 (emperor) 拉 (pull) 下 (down) 马 (horse)
column5:
他做事有种舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的劲儿。 / He has the kind of nerve to risk it all and take down anyone.
舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马，揭发腐败需要这种勇气。 / Exposing corruption takes that kind of risk-it-all courage.
记者就该有舍得一身剐，敢把皇帝拉下马的精神。 / Journalists should have that fearless spirit to challenge the powerful.
column6: Bold proverb about daring to challenge power despite great risk.

column2: mō zhe shí tou guò hé
column3: cross the river by feeling the stones
column4: 摸 (feel) 着 (-ing) 石 (stone) 头 (suffix) 过 (cross) 河 (river)
column5:
这是新业务，只能摸着石头过河。 / This is a new business — we can only feel our way forward.
改革就是摸着石头过河，一步一步来。 / Reform means feeling for stones — going step by step.
第一次当父母，都是摸着石头过河。 / First-time parents are all just figuring it out as they go.
column6: Proceed cautiously through trial and error.

column3: to worry unnecessarily
column4: 杞 (Qi state) 人 (man) 忧 (worry) 天 (sky)
column5:
你别杞人忧天了，没那么严重。 / Stop worrying about nothing — it's not that serious.
他总是杞人忧天，担心一堆不会发生的事。 / He's always fretting over things that won't happen.
这种担心纯属杞人忧天。 / This kind of worry is completely unfounded.
column6: Literally a man of Qi worrying the sky will fall; imaginary/unnecessary anxiety.

column3: draw a snake and add feet / overdo it
column4: 画 (draw) 蛇 (snake) 添 (add) 足 (foot)
column5:
你这报告已经很完整，再加内容就是画蛇添足了。 / Your report is already complete — adding more would be overkill.
解释太多反而画蛇添足。 / Over-explaining only ruins the effect.
这个设计本来挺好，加这个就画蛇添足了。 / The design was fine — adding this just makes it worse.
column6: From a fable — adding superfluous detail ruins the work. Used when extra effort backfires.

column3: wait by the stump for a rabbit
column4: 守 (guard) 株 (stump) 待 (wait) 兔 (rabbit)
column5:
你不主动找工作，光守株待兔可不行。 / You can't just sit and wait for a job — you have to look.
做生意不能守株待兔，得想办法。 / In business you can't sit and wait — you have to take action.
他天天守株待兔，希望机会从天上掉下来。 / He waits around every day hoping opportunity will fall from the sky.
column6: Fable: farmer saw one rabbit hit a stump, then quit farming to wait. Means passive reliance on luck.

column3: old frontier-man lost his horse / blessing in disguise
column4: 塞 (frontier) 翁 (old man) 失 (lose) 马 (horse)
column5:
丢了工作也别难过，塞翁失马焉知非福。 / Don't be sad about losing the job — it might be a blessing in disguise.
这事看似不好，可塞翁失马，谁知道呢？ / It seems bad now, but every cloud has a silver lining — who knows?
塞翁失马的故事告诉我们要看长远。 / The story of the lost horse teaches us to take the long view.
column6: Often paired with 焉知非福 — bad luck may be good in disguise.

column3: one stroke, two gains / kill two birds
column4: 一 (one) 举 (act) 两 (two) 得 (gain)
column5:
骑车上班既省钱又锻炼，真是一举两得。 / Biking to work saves money and gives exercise — two birds, one stone.
这个方法一举两得，太聪明了。 / This approach kills two birds with one stone — brilliant.
学中文还能交朋友，可以说是一举两得。 / Learning Chinese while making friends — you get two benefits at once.
column6: Very practical. Same idea as English "two birds, one stone".

column3: when in Rome, do as the Romans
column4: 入 (enter) 乡 (village) 随 (follow) 俗 (custom)
column5:
来中国就入乡随俗，多用筷子吧。 / In China, do as the locals do — use chopsticks more.
出国旅游要入乡随俗，尊重当地文化。 / When traveling abroad, follow local customs and respect their culture.
他刚到日本就开始入乡随俗。 / As soon as he got to Japan he started following local customs.
column6: Literally "enter village, follow customs". Daily-life usable.

column3: self-contradictory
column4: 自 (self) 相 (mutually) 矛 (spear) 盾 (shield)
column5:
他的话前后自相矛盾。 / What he said contradicts itself.
这个政策有点自相矛盾。 / This policy is somewhat self-contradictory.
你刚才的解释自相矛盾，我没听懂。 / Your explanation contradicted itself — I didn't follow.
column6: From a tale of a seller hawking an unstoppable spear and an unbreakable shield.

column3: quench thirst by gazing at plums / false hope
column4: 望 (gaze) 梅 (plum) 止 (stop) 渴 (thirst)
column5:
光想着升职是望梅止渴，得实际行动。 / Just dreaming of a promotion is wishful thinking — you need action.
看美食视频对减肥的人来说是望梅止渴。 / For dieters, watching food videos is just imagined relief.
他靠回忆从前的成功来望梅止渴。 / He comforts himself by recalling past successes — a hollow consolation.
column6: Cao Cao tricked thirsty troops by promising plums ahead. Means consoling with imagination.

column3: give up halfway
column4: 半 (half) 途 (way) 而 (and) 废 (abandon)
column5:
学了一半就放弃，太可惜了，别半途而废。 / It's a shame to quit halfway — don't give up midway.
做事最忌讳半途而废。 / The worst thing in any task is quitting halfway.
他这个人做什么都半途而废。 / He gives up halfway on everything he does.
column6: Common warning against quitting partway through.

column3: dripping water pierces stone / persistence wins
column4: 水 (water) 滴 (drip) 石 (stone) 穿 (pierce)
column5:
每天背十个单词，水滴石穿，一年就很多了。 / Ten words a day adds up — persistence pierces stone.
学习就像水滴石穿，贵在坚持。 / Learning is like water wearing through stone — the key is persistence.
水滴石穿的道理大家都懂，但能做到的不多。 / Everyone knows persistence pays, but few actually do it.
column6: Classic image for slow, steady effort beating brute force.

column3: frog at the bottom of a well / narrow worldview
column4: 井 (well) 底 (bottom) 之 (of) 蛙 (frog)
column5:
不出去看看，永远是井底之蛙。 / If you never go out and see, you'll always be a frog in a well.
别做井底之蛙，多了解外面的世界。 / Don't be narrow-minded — learn more about the world out there.
他出国后才意识到自己以前是井底之蛙。 / After going abroad he realized how narrow his view had been.
column6: Insult-ish: someone whose view of the world is tiny.

column2: dī shuǐ zhī ēn dāng yǒng quán xiāng bào
column3: repay a drop of kindness with a gushing spring
column4: 滴 (drip) 水 (water) 之 (of) 恩 (kindness) 当 (should) 涌 (gush) 泉 (spring) 相 (mutually) 报 (repay)
column5:
他常说滴水之恩当涌泉相报，从不忘恩。 / He often says a small kindness deserves great repayment — he never forgets a favor.
我帮过他一次，他却滴水之恩当涌泉相报。 / I helped him once, and he repaid me many times over.
中国人讲究滴水之恩，当涌泉相报。 / Chinese culture values repaying small kindness with great generosity.
column6: Classical gratitude proverb. Strong literary tone.

column2: zhī jǐ zhī bǐ bǎi zhàn bú dài
column3: know yourself and your enemy — never lose
column4: 知 (know) 己 (self) 彼 (other) 百 (hundred) 战 (battle) 不 (not) 殆 (peril)
column5:
做生意要知己知彼，百战不殆。 / In business, know yourself and your competitor and you'll always win.
面试前先了解公司，知己知彼，百战不殆。 / Research the company before the interview — knowing both sides ensures success.
知己知彼，百战不殆，这是孙子兵法的智慧。 / Know yourself and your enemy — this is the wisdom of Sun Tzu.
column6: From Sunzi Art of War. Still quoted in modern speech.
